presentation.title: Adriano Andrade da Silva
presentation.openToWork: Open to Senior Full Stack / Front-End roles
presentation.summary:
7 years of software development experience, with 3 years owning architectural decisions on React/TypeScript frontend systems serving millions of monthly users — responsible for defining technical standards, delivery pace, and cross-team alignment.

Specialist in component architecture, AdTech integrations, design systems, and performance engineering.

Proven track record of real-world impact: replaced a third-party FAQ system with an in-house solution cutting operational costs, and delivered automation tooling adopted across the Operations team.
presentation.about: Corinthians fan, music and POP culture enthusiast.

experience.title: Experience
experience.filter: Filter by technology (e.g.: React, Node, Ruby)

jobs.name: UOL
jobs.description:
Architecture & High-Impact Frontend
Progressed from feature delivery to owning system-wide architecture decisions and driving technical alignment across multiple business units. Architected and led the evolution of React/TypeScript applications on platforms handling millions of monthly visits — prioritizing scalability, maintainability, and performance. Defined componentization standards, global state contracts, and module structures for multidisciplinary teams.

AdTech & Programmatic Advertising
Integrated Google Ad Manager, Prebid, and proprietary Ad Server infrastructure, implementing bidding logic and ad inventory management across high-traffic portals. Ensured high availability and consistent performance under variable load.

Design System & Component Architecture
Architected reusable component libraries aligned to design systems, collaborating directly with UX/UI teams (Figma, Adobe XD, Zeplin). Reduced visual inconsistencies and accelerated delivery cycles for new interfaces.

Cross-team Collaboration
Worked closely with Data (Analytics), Backend teams from other business areas, and Infrastructure to deliver robust, integrated solutions and ensure alignment with company-wide technical strategies.

Performance & Quality
Optimized critical applications with focus on code splitting, lazy loading, and Web Vitals. Implemented automated testing with Vitest, improving reliability and code coverage across large-scale codebases.

Automation & Internal Systems
Led development of UOL's new FAQ system, replacing a third-party solution and significantly cutting operational costs. Designed automation tooling for bulk ad campaign publishing, adopted by Operations teams.

jobs.name: Gigaservices
jobs.description:
Interfaces & Full Stack Development
Developed and maintained views in Ruby on Rails, applying JavaScript, jQuery, and Bootstrap to deliver functional and responsive interfaces aligned to product requirements.

Backend & Database
Implemented new features in Ruby with PostgreSQL and RSpec test coverage, contributing to system stability and continuous evolution.

Quality & Support
Actively collaborated in testing cycles, identifying and resolving technical issues with agility and focus on customer experience.

items.name: Portfolio
items.problem: Build a technical presence that communicates engineering seniority and real decisions — not just a list of technologies used.
items.solution: Architected with Next.js App Router, with full component isolation, native i18n without external dependencies, and dark mode via next-themes.
items.techDecisions: Context API for minimal, predictable global state; shadcn/ui + Tailwind for a consistent design system without overhead; SSR/SSG for performance and SEO from the first request.
items.outcome: Bilingual portfolio (PT/EN) with dark mode and responsive sidebar navigation. Shipped to production with full TypeScript coverage, zero external i18n dependencies, and architectural decisions documented across every project card.

items.name: Formula One Database
items.problem: Lack of a structured, typed, and reliable API with up-to-date Formula 1 data to power a real frontend application.
items.solution: Designed and implemented a REST API in Node.js with a layered architecture, typed and documented endpoints — serving as the data contract for the frontend.
items.techDecisions: Express for lightweight control over the request pipeline; TypeScript to enforce explicit contracts between layers; strict separation across routes -> controllers -> data, enabling independent evolution and maintenance.
items.outcome: Production API consumed by Formula One Next, with low layer coupling, extensible structure for new season data, and full TypeScript contracts ensuring safe changes across all endpoints.

items.name: Formula One Next
items.problem: Build a rich, performant, and navigable interface for displaying Formula 1 data sourced from a custom API.
items.solution: Next.js application with selective SSR and SSG per route, integrating the Formula One Database API with a focus on performance and SEO.
items.techDecisions: Next.js for per-route hybrid rendering; Styled Components for style isolation without global conflicts; Bootstrap as a responsive grid without adding unnecessary complexity.
items.outcome: Published application with optimized load times, responsive layout, and a clear separation between data and presentation via server-side rendering.

contact.title: Contact
contact.ctaDescription: Available for Senior Full Stack / Front-End opportunities (remote or hybrid, GMT-3). Will respond within 24h.

mindset.title: Engineering Mindset
mindset.subtitle: How I approach technical problems, architecture decisions, and systems engineering day-to-day.

items.title: Architecture before code
items.description: Before writing any line, I evaluate design trade-offs: separation of concerns, contracts between layers, and how the solution holds up as the system grows. In practice: the FAQ system replacement started with an architecture spike that defined clear integration boundaries before any code was written, reducing rework throughout the entire delivery.

items.title: Performance as a requirement, not an afterthought
items.description: Performance is part of the design from day one. Code splitting, lazy loading, selective memoization, and continuous Web Vitals monitoring are built into development — not patched in later. On UOL's high-traffic portals, this approach maintained consistent performance metrics even under variable ad load and dynamic content.

items.title: Design System & Componentization
items.description: Components are contracts. I prioritize reusable primitives with a clear API, predictable behavior, and design system alignment — reducing rework and accelerating delivery.

items.title: Strong typing as a design tool
items.description: TypeScript is not just about preventing bugs — it is living documentation of the system. Well-defined types communicate intent, reduce friction in code reviews, and make large-scale refactors safe.

items.title: Testability as a design criterion
items.description: Hard-to-test code is a design smell. I write tests that validate behavior, not implementation — using coverage as a quality signal, not a target.

items.title: Documentation scales knowledge
items.description: Well-written ADRs, READMEs, and API contracts multiply team knowledge. Good documentation reduces onboarding cost and increases delivery velocity.
